name: the primitive type called peep

phase_0.boundary_condition: The quantum coherence volume defining the lossless fidelity threshold

VINIT.justification: The vacuum state before any fidelity measurement or type assignment occurs

TANCH.justification: The terminal boundary where quantum information is either preserved or lost to the environment

AFWD.justification: The forward evolution of the quantum state toward a high-fidelity target

AREV.justification: The reverse transformation required to verify state reversibility and fidelity

CLINK.justification: The sequential chaining of quantum operations to form a complex circuit

IMSCRIB.justification: The identity operation where the state recognizes its own coherence

FSPLIT.justification: The division of a coherent state into orthogonal basis components for evaluation

FFUSE.justification: The interference-driven reconstitution of the original state from its branches

EVALT.justification: The confirmation of quantum/lossless fidelity (ℏ) exceeding the threshold

EVALF.justification: The failure to maintain lossless status, resulting in classical lossy (age) state

ENGAGR.justification: A state held in simultaneous fidelity modes before measurement resolution

IFIX.justification: The permanent fixation of the fidelity result into the classical observer log

phase_3.false_description: fidelity loss detected, state reverted to classical

steps.domain_action: initialize the computational manifold for fidelity assessment
identify the target state as a candidate for the peep primitive
apply unitary evolution to prepare the high-fidelity encoding
branch the state into parallel evaluation paths for fidelity verification
confirm the state maintains quantum lossless characteristics (ℏ)
compose the verified state with the global fidelity lattice
reconstitute the branched state into a unified coherent object
perform a reverse check to ensure no information was lost during fusion
hold the state in a paradice of potentiality before final fixation
record the peep primitive assignment into the permanent type registry
seal the coherence volume to prevent external decoherence

phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The quantum clock cycle governing gate application

phase_6.cycle_cost: Zero-point energy fluctuations within the coherence volume
